data.title: Renewed Peace Talks Weigh On Oil Prices
data.description:
By Warren Patterson, Head of Commodities Strategy and Ewa Manthey, Commodities Strategist

Reports that the US and Russia are working on a peace plan for Ukraine put downward pressure on oil and European gas prices yesterday.

Energy: Russia-Ukraine peace talk noise

Oil prices came under pressure yesterday, with ICE Brent settling about 2.1% lower. Some of the downward pressure is derived from reports that the US and Russia are working on a new peace plan for Ukraine.

However, with suggestions that the plan is favourable towards Russia, it may be unlikely that Ukraine will back it. Signs that the US is still trying to work on a deal ease some concerns over further sanctions against Russia and also how strongly current curbs will be enforced.

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) released its weekly US inventory data yesterday, reporting that US commercial crude oil inventories fell by 3.43m barrels over the week. The decline was driven by stronger crude exports, which grew 1.34m b/d week on week.

Refiners also increased run rates, leading to stronger crude oil inputs. Stronger run rates shouldn’t be too surprising, given the strength in refinery margins, while refiners are also exiting maintenance season. Changes in inventory for refined products were more bearish.

Gasoline inventories increased by 2.33m barrels, while distillate stocks edged up by 171k barrels. Implied gasoline demand was also weaker, falling by 500k b/d WoW. The more bearish gasoline numbers saw the Reformulated Blendstock for Oxygenate Blending (RBOB) crack coming under some pressure yesterday.

European gas prices also traded lower yesterday, with the Title Transfer Facility (TTF) settling almost 2.4% lower. Reports of peace talks between Russia and the US weighed on prices. However, colder weather has led to gas storage falling at a faster pace over the last few days.

EU gas storage is now 81% full, down from a peak of just over 83% in mid-October. Meanwhile, the latest positioning data shows that investment funds cut their net long in TTF by 8.7TWh to 15.6TWh, the smallest net long since March 2024.

This was driven by fresh shorts entering the market, with the gross short rising by 19TWh to 434.3TWh, yet another record gross short. It leaves the market vulnerable to a short-covering rally in the event of any supply surprises or extended cold snaps over the winter.

Metals: Chile lifts copper price forecasts

Chile, the biggest copper producer, raised its price forecasts for this year and next. The country’s copper agency, Cochilco, cited global supply disruptions, lower interest rates, a weaker dollar and global economic resilience.

Copper is expected to average $4.45 a pound this year and $4.55 in 2026, according to Cochilco’s quarterly report. It previously forecast $4.30 for both this year and next. Copper supply has been hit by a wave of disruptions this year, including an accident at the El Teniente mine in Chile in July.

Cochilco now expects no production growth in Chile for the year, down from a previous forecast of 1.5%. In 2026, Chilean production is forecast to grow 2.5% to 5.6 million metric tonnes. However, a key assumption about next year's growth is that El Teniente will operate as normal, which Codelco says is unlikely.

Agriculture: Sugar and ethanol dynamics

Sugar prices rose yesterday before giving back all of these gains. Reports that India is considering raising ethanol prices provided support, as it could mean more sugar being diverted to ethanol production, helping to eat into the large global surplus expected. Meanwhile, in Brazil, mills should also be moving to favor ethanol over sugar production, with sugar prices trading below ethanol parity.

Content Disclaimer

This publication has been prepared by ING solely for information purposes irrespective of a particular user's means, financial situation or investment objectives. The information does not constitute investment recommendation, and nor is it investment, legal or tax advice or an offer or solicitation to purchase or sell any financial instrument. Read more

Original Post

data.title: Tracking Seth Klarman's Baupost Group Holdings - Q3 2025 Update
data.description:
This article is part of a series that provides an ongoing analysis of the changes made to Baupost Group's 13F stock portfolio on a quarterly basis. It is based on Klarman’s regulatory 13F Form filed on 11/13/2025. Please visit our Tracking Seth Klarman’s Baupost Group Holdings article for an idea of how his holdings have progressed over the years and our previous update for the fund's moves during Q2 2025.

Baupost Group’s 13F portfolio value increased from ~$4.11B to ~$4.76B this quarter. The total number of 13F securities decreased from 20 to 19. The portfolio is heavily concentrated with Restaurant Brands, Alphabet, Elevance Health, CRH plc, and Willis Towers Watson together accounting for ~46% of the 13F holdings.

Since its inception (1982), Baupost Group’s 13F portfolio has accounted for between 2.4% and 15% of the Assets Under Management (AUM). The current allocation is at the high end of that range. The rest of the AUM is diversified among cash, debt, real estate, and hedges. On average, the fund has held ~25% cash over the last decade. Seth Klarman’s distinct investment style is elaborated in his 1991 book, “Margin of Safety: Risk-averse value investing strategies for the thoughtful investor.” The book is out-of-print, and copies sell for a huge premium.

New Stakes:

Union Pacific (UNP): UNP is a large 7.43% of the portfolio position purchased this quarter at prices between ~$211 and ~$241, and the stock currently trades at ~$221.

Genuine Parts (GPC): The ~4% GPC stake was established this quarter at prices between ~$120 and ~$144. The stock is now at ~$125.

Americold Realty Trust (COLD): COLD is a very small 0.93% of the portfolio position purchased this quarter at prices between ~$12 and ~$17. The stock is now trading below that range at $10.40.

Stake Disposals:

Liberty Broadband (LBRDK): The 1.79% LBRDK stake was built during the last quarter at prices between ~$71 and ~$104. The disposal this quarter was at prices between ~$58 and ~$102. The stock currently trades at $44.88.

Viasat (VSAT): VSAT was 3.25% of the portfolio position. Klarman first purchased VSAT in 2008 at a cost basis in the high teens. In July 2020, Baupost participated in ViaSat’s 4.47M share private placement by acquiring ~2.56M shares at ~$39 per share. Q3 2024 saw ~11% selling at prices between $11.94 and $24.49. That was followed by a ~17% reduction during Q1 2025 at prices between $7.45 and $12.55. The position was sold this quarter at prices between ~$14 and ~$34. The stock currently trades at $34.92.

ICON plc (ICLR): ICLR was 1.43% of the portfolio position purchased during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$168 and ~$228. The elimination this quarter was at prices between ~$139 and ~$211. The stock is now at ~$159.

Amcor plc (AMCR): The 1.22% AMCR stake was established during the last quarter at prices between $8.37 and $9.82. It was sold this quarter at prices between $7.90 and $10. It is now at $8.42.

Stake Increases:

Restaurant Brands (QSR): The 11.11% QSR stake is now their largest 13F position. It was established during Q4 2024 at prices between ~$65 and ~$73. There was a one-third stake increase during the next quarter at prices between ~$60 and ~$70. This quarter saw the position more than double at prices between ~$61 and ~$72. The stock is now at $67.83.

Elevance Health (ELV): The ~9% ELV stake was established during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$365 and ~$439. The last quarter saw a ~150% stake increase at prices between ~$357 and ~$459. That was followed by an ~115% stake increase this quarter at prices between ~$274 and ~$401. The stock is now at ~$316.

Ferguson Enterprises (FERG): FERG is a fairly large 5.45% of the portfolio position purchased during Q4 2024 at prices between ~$174 and ~$222. The stock currently trades at ~$237. There was a minor ~2% stake increase this quarter.

Eagle Materials (EXP): EXP is a 4.60% of the portfolio stake established during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$196 and ~$272. The next quarter saw a ~30% stake increase at prices between ~$213 and ~$274. Q3 2024 saw a ~45% stake increase at prices between ~$212 and ~$292. That was followed by a ~28% stake increase during the next quarter at prices between $246.48 and $315. Q1 2025 saw a ~43% further increase at prices between ~$205 and ~$265. There was a ~25% selling during the last quarter at prices between ~$192 and ~$243. This quarter saw a ~40% stake increase at prices between ~$200 and ~$243. The stock currently trades at ~$204.

Stake Decreases:

Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL): The 9.50% of the portfolio GOOGL stake is currently the second largest 13F position. It was purchased during Q1 2020 at prices between ~$53 and ~$76. The position has wavered. Recent activity follows. Q2 2024 saw ~65% selling at prices between ~$152 and ~$187. There was a ~37% stake increase during the next quarter at prices between ~$150 and ~$192. That was followed by a ~46% stake increase during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$152 and ~$209. The last quarter saw a ~27% further increase at prices between ~$143 and ~$183. There was a ~30% selling this quarter at prices between ~$173 and ~$256. The stock is now at ~$293.

CRH plc (CRH): The 8.52% CRH stake was primarily built during Q3 2023 at prices between ~$53 and ~$60. The stake was decreased by 19% during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$66 and ~$87. The next quarter saw a ~55% stake increase at prices between ~$73 and ~$86. Q3 2024 saw a ~38% reduction at prices between ~$72 and ~$93. There was a ~42% stake increase during the last quarter at prices between ~$77 and ~$100. This quarter saw a ~12% trimming at prices between ~$91 and ~$122. The stock currently trades at ~$110.

Willis Towers Watson (WTW): WTW is currently at 7.90% of the portfolio. It was established during Q1 2021 at prices between ~$200 and ~$235. The stake has wavered. Recent activity follows. There was a ~17% selling during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$303 and ~$344. That was followed by a ~14% reduction during the last quarter at prices between ~$293 and ~$341. This quarter also saw ~17% selling at prices between ~$298 and ~$346. The stock currently trades at ~$317.

WESCO International (WCC): The 6.67% WCC position was purchased during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$133 and ~$194. The next quarter saw the stake built at prices between ~$152 and ~$191. Liberty Global (LBTYK): LBTYK is currently at 6.56% of the portfolio. That was followed by a ~10% trimming this quarter at prices between $9.95 and $12.32. The stock currently trades at $11.02.

Note: The prices quoted above are adjusted for the Sunrise (OTC:SNREY) spinoff in November 2024.

Kept Steady:

Dollar General (DG): DG is 5.79% of the portfolio position purchased during Q3 2024 at prices between ~$80 and ~$132, and the stock currently trades at ~$100. There was an ~11% trimming during Q4 2024 at prices between ~$73 and ~$83. The last quarter saw a ~27% stake increase at prices between ~$85 and ~$111.

Fidelity National Information Services (FIS): FIS is 5.25% of the portfolio position purchased during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$67 and ~$84, and the stock currently trades below that range at $62.66. There was an ~9% stake increase during the last quarter.

GDS Holdings (GDS): The 2.64% GDS stake saw a ~65% stake increase during Q2 2024 at prices between ~$6 and ~$10. There was a ~24% selling during the last quarter at prices between ~$17 and ~$31. The stock currently trades at $29.75.

Fiserv, Inc. (FISV): FISV is 2.42% of the portfolio position purchased during the last quarter at prices between ~$156 and ~$227, and the stock currently trades well below that range at $61.17.

Herbalife (HLF): The 1.38% HLF position was increased by 34% during Q1 2024 at prices between $8.03 and $15.36. That was followed by a ~130% stake increase during the next quarter at prices between $7.65 and $11.70. Q4 2024 saw another ~50% stake increase at prices between $6.33 and $8.67. There was a ~225% further increase during the next quarter at prices between $5.04 and $9.23. The stock is now at $9.22.

PagSeguro Digital (PAGS): PAGS is a small 0.52% of the portfolio position purchased during the last quarter at prices between $7.36 and $10.21, and it now goes for $9.91.
